content.problemStatement:
.

Chef offers delicious food in his restaurant, but he wants to make it a more attractive place too.

The guests in the restaurant usually walk along a path consisting of two adjacent, parallel rows of square bricks leading to the entrance. Each row is NNN bricks long, all bricks have the same size and there are no holes.

Unfortunately, the path looks pretty boring because all bricks have the same color. In order to make it more interesting, Chef wants to replace exactly KKK bricks by more beautiful ones in such a way that no two replaced bricks share a side.

Chef asks you to answer a question that is too complicated for him: in how many ways can Chef select the bricks to replace? Since the answer can be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of ways to replace bricks, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are exactly 8 valid ways to choose the positions of K=2 beautiful bricks.
There are exactly 24 valid ways to choose the positions of K=2 beautiful bricks.
There are exactly 80 valid ways to choose the positions of K=3 beautiful bricks.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use combinatorial reasoning and modular arithmetic. We need to calculate the number of ways to choose K bricks out of 2N bricks such that no two chosen bricks share a side. This can be done by considering the bricks as a sequence and choosing K positions out of 2N positions such that no two chosen positions are adjacent. We can use the formula for combinations with repetition to calculate this. The time complexity is O(T*K) and the space complexity is O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*K) — per query is O(K), total is O(T*K)

code.Python3:
MOD = 1000000007

def powmod(a, b):
    res = 1
    while b > 0:
        if b & 1: res = (res * a) % MOD
        a = (a * a) % MOD
        b >>= 1
    return res

def inv(a):
    return powmod(a, MOD - 2)

def comb(n, k):
    num = 1
    den = 1
    for i in range(1, k + 1):
        num = (num * (n - i + 1)) % MOD
        den = (den * i) % MOD
    return (num * inv(den)) % MOD

t = int(input())
while t > 0:
    n, k = map(int, input().split())
    ans = 0
    for i in range(k + 1):
        ways = comb(n - k + 1, i) * comb(n - i, k - i)
        ways %= MOD
        ans = (ans + ways) % MOD
    print(ans)
    t -= 1
